title: FSMA Produce Safety — Agricultural Water Microbial Quality
text: Pre-harvest agricultural water (water that directly contacts produce or food contact surfaces) microbial quality: geometric mean ≤ 126 CFU generic E. coli per 100 mL + statistical threshold value ≤ 410 CFU per 100 mL. Untreated surface water — initial 20 samples; rolling 5 samples annually. Treated water + ground water — fewer samples. Corrective measures if exceeded.

title: FSMA Produce Safety — Worker Training + Health/Hygiene
text: Workers handling covered produce + food contact surfaces must be trained on: principles of food hygiene; recognition of symptoms of illness; cleaning + sanitation; handwashing technique. Workers w/ infectious disease symptoms (vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, infected wounds) must not handle covered produce. Documented training.

title: FSMA Produce Safety — Domesticated + Wild Animals
text: Operations must monitor for evidence of animals (feces, footprints, intrusion) in growing areas, especially close to harvest. If significant evidence of contamination, evaluate + take corrective action: do not harvest contaminated portion, increase buffer, ensure handwashing. Document monitoring activities.

title: Pesticide labeling — REI + PHI + MRL
text: Pesticide labels are legal documents. REI (Restricted Entry Interval) — workers may not enter treated area for X hours after application (typically 4-48 hr). PHI (Preharvest Interval) — minimum days between last application + harvest. MRL (Maximum Residue Level) — codified in 40 CFR Part 180. Off-label use = federal violation. Worker Protection Standard (29 CFR 170).

title: NOP — Land Requirements for Organic
text: Organic crop production land must: have no prohibited substance applied for 3 years before harvest; have distinct, defined boundaries + buffer zones sufficient to prevent contamination from adjoining land (chemical drift, contaminated water runoff); be managed per Organic System Plan (OSP) approved annually by certifier.

title: NOP — Facility Pest Management Practice Standard
text: Permitted in order: removal of pest habitat, food sources, breeding sites; mechanical or physical controls (traps, light, sound); lures + repellents using nonsynthetic substances or NOP National List approved synthetics; nonsynthetic + NOP approved synthetic pesticides ONLY if previous fail. Prohibited synthetic pesticide application = loss of organic certification.

title: NOP — Commingling + Contact w/ Prohibited Substances
text: Split-operation handlers (organic + non-organic) must: physically separate organic + non-organic during production, handling, storage; prevent commingling at all stages; prevent contact w/ prohibited substances (cleaning agents, sanitizers, packaging); maintain audit trail. Documentation showing organic identity preservation throughout. Detected commingling = lot suspension.

title: GlobalGAP — Integrated Farm Assurance v6
text: Crops Base + Fruit&Vegetables modules. Major Musts (100% required), Minor Musts (95% required), Recommendations. Topics: site history, propagation material, soil management, fertilizer, water, plant protection (PHI, RUP, calibration), harvest, post-harvest, traceability + recall, worker H+S, environmental. Annual third-party audit.

title: International Labor Org child-labor standards
text: Minimum age for employment 15 (Conv 138); 18 for hazardous work (Conv 182). Hazardous work in agriculture: pesticide handling, heavy machinery operation, climbing trees > 2m, work > 8h/day. Exception: family-farm light work for ≥ 12yo under parental supervision (US: similar in 29 USC §213(c) FLSA agricultural exemption — narrowly interpreted).

title: HACCP for juice processing
text: Juice manufacturers must implement HACCP w/ Critical Control Points achieving 5-log reduction of pertinent pathogens. Common CCPs: pasteurization (time/temp), UV treatment (UV-C dose), antimicrobial treatment, pH control. Records: monitoring + verification + corrective actions. Sanitation SOPs + recall plan.
